rationale: Add a small procedural config to force shorter inline CLI arguments by preferring temp files and chunking, reducing Windows arg-length risk without touching other files.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.cli_arg_guard.v1",
  "prefer_temp_files": true,
  "max_inline_chars": 4096,
  "chunk_size_chars": 2048,
  "notes": "Avoid long inline args on Windows; use temp files for large payloads."
}

risks: Low. New config may be unused until wired; no runtime behavior change if not referenced.
expected_benefit: Provides a standard guardrail for future command construction to reduce Windows CLI arg-length failures.
